title: Ensure customer created Customer Managed Key (CMK) is  rotated at least annually

input_row.title: Ensure customer created Customer Managed Key (CMK) is  rotated at least annually
input_row.description: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Vault securely stores master encryption keys that protect your encrypted data. You can use the Vault service to rotate keys to generate new cryptographic material. Periodically rotating keys limits the amount of data encrypted by one key version.
input_row.rationale: Rotating keys annually limits the data encrypted under one key version. Key rotation thereby reduces the risk in case a key is ever compromised.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Login into OCI Console. 2. Select Identity & Security from the Services menu. 3. Select Vault. 4. Click on the individual Vault under the Name heading. 5. Ensure the date of each Master Encryption key under the Created column of the Master Encryption key is no more than 365 days old, and that the key is in the ENABLED state 6. Repeat for all Vaults in all compartments From CLI: 1. Execute the following for each Vault in each compartment oci kms management key list --compartment-id '' --endpoint '' --all --query "data[*].[\"time- created\",\"display-name\",\"lifecycle-state\"]" 2. Ensure the date of the Master Encryption key is no more than 365 days old and is also in the ENABLED state.
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Login into OCI Console. 2. Select Identity & Security from the Services menu. 3. Select Vault. 4. Click on the individual Vault under the Name heading. 5. Click on the menu next to the time created. 6. Click Rotate Key From CLI: 1. Execute the following: oci kms management key rotate --key-id --endpoint
input_row.justification: The control requires checking the rotation status of customer-managed keys, which can be effectively automated using AWS APIs. AWS provides the necessary capabilities through the AWS CLI and boto3 to list keys and verify their rotation status. The second AI opinion correctly identifies the use of AWS KMS APIs for this purpose, which aligns with AWS's capabilities to automate such checks.
input_row.automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: AWS KMS (Key Management Service) - API/CLI Method: - AWS CLI: `aws kms list-keys` to retrieve key IDs, followed by `aws kms get-key-rotation-status --key-id <key-id>` to check rotation status. - Boto3: Use `client.list_keys()` to get key IDs and `client.get_key_rotation_status(KeyId=<key-id>)` to verify rotation status. - Properties to Check: `KeyRotationEnabled` attribute - Pass Criteria: `KeyRotationEnabled` is `True` for all customer-managed keys. - Fail Criteria: Any key with `KeyRotationEnabled` set to `False` or not checked within the last 365 days.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** oci_kms_key_cmk_rotation_annual_compliance

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **OCI Resource/Service:** OCI Key Management (Vault and Keys)
- **OCI Python SDK / OCI CLI Methods:**
    - Enumerate all compartments.
    - For each compartment, list all Vaults using: `oci kms management vault list --compartment-id <compartment_id> --all`
    - For each Vault, obtain the Management endpoint URL.
    - List all keys in the vault using: `oci kms management key list --compartment-id <compartment_id> --endpoint <vault_management_endpoint> --all`
    - For each customer-managed key (CMK), fetch:
        - `time-created` (timestamp of key creation or most recent rotation/version)
        - `display-name`
        - `lifecycle-state` (must be `ENABLED`)
        - List all key versions: `oci kms management key-version list --key-id <key_id> --endpoint <vault_management_endpoint>`
        - Identify the latest/active key version and its creation date.
    - **Evaluation Logic:**
        - For every CMK in `ENABLED` state, check the `time-created` (either of the key itself or its latest key version, whichever represents most recent rotation).
        - Ensure this date is no older than 365 days from the audit date.
- **Pass Criteria:** All customer-managed keys (CMK) in all vaults, in all compartments:
    - In `ENABLED` state
    - Most recent rotation (latest key version's `time-created`) is within the last 365 days
- **Fail Criteria:** Any ENABLED CMK for which:
    - The most recent rotation (creation time of latest key version) is older than 365 days
    - Or the key is not in the `ENABLED` state (these typically do not require rotation, but state should be checked)
- **Example OCI CLI Command:**
    - `oci kms management key list --compartment-id <compartment_id> --endpoint <vault_management_endpoint> --all --query "data[*].[\"time-created\",\"display-name\",\"lifecycle-state\"]"`
    - For each key, if required, `oci kms management key-version list --key-id <key_id> --endpoint <vault_management_endpoint> --all` to check last rotated date.
- **Note:** To be fully exhaustive, enumerate keys across all relevant compartments and vaults. Ensure that assessment discards Oracle-managed keys (check Key `protection_mode` or `managed_by` if required - only customer-managed keys are in scope).
- **Documentation Reference:** [OCI Vault Documentation – Rotating Master Encryption Keys](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/KeyManagement/Tasks/rotatingkeys.htm)

**SOURCE:** CIS_Oracle_Cloud_Infrastructure_Foundations_Benchmark_v3_0_0
